meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kits to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Master SaaS starter kits to ship faster and eliminate bugs using expert-level workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop wasting hours manually hunting syntax errors. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you inherit pre-configured, bug-free architecture. Combine this with Antigravity for automated refactoring and Claude Code for intelligent CLI debugging to transform your development cycle from a trial-and-error slog into a repeatable, high-velocity professional pipeline.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and you’re staring at a cryptic stack trace that’s been haunting your terminal for three hours. You aren't just losing time; you're losing your reputation. The 'can't debug code' loop is the silent killer of every freelance career, turning profitable projects into bottomless pits of frustration.

But elite builders don't fight fires; they build fireproof systems. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you effectively outsource the boilerplate complexity to battle-tested patterns. When you pair this architecture with AI-driven debugging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you move from being a coder fighting syntax to an architect orchestrating scalable, production-ready software.

steps.title: Bootstrap with a Hardened Starter Kit
steps.description: Initialize a project using a vetted SaaS boilerplate that includes pre-configured authentication, database schemas, and global error handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project structure using the latest production-grade boilerplate patterns. Ensure strict TypeScript configuration, absolute imports, and a modular folder structure that enforces separation of concerns.

steps.title: Automate Dependency Health Checks
steps.description: Prevent 'dependency hell' by using AI to audit your package versions and identify conflicting peer dependencies before they break your build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan the current project dependencies for known vulnerabilities and version conflicts. Generate a report of necessary updates and suggest a migration path for breaking changes.

steps.title: Implement AI-Assisted Error Tracing
steps.description: When a runtime error occurs, pipe the stack trace directly into Claude Code to isolate the root cause instead of manual logging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following stack trace and identify the specific file and line number causing the memory leak. Provide a refactored code block that fixes the issue while maintaining existing business logic.

steps.title: Automate Integration Testing
steps.description: Generate suite-based tests for your API endpoints to ensure that new code doesn't break existing SaaS features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of Playwright integration tests for the authentication and payment gateway modules. Focus on edge cases including invalid tokens and network timeouts.

steps.title: Refactor for Performance and Clarity
steps.description: Use AI to clean up technical debt and ensure your codebase remains maintainable for future client handoffs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for 'code smell' and unnecessary complexity. Propose a refactor that improves readability and reduces cyclomatic complexity without changing output behavior.

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'golden branch' in your starter kit that only contains verified, bug-free core features.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation comments for every complex utility function—this saves hours during client handoffs.
Configure your CI/CD pipeline to run an 'AI-audit' step on every pull request to catch logic errors before they hit staging.
When debugging, provide the AI with the 'context of change' rather than just the error; it significantly improves the accuracy of the fix.
Treat your SaaS starter kit as a product; iterate on it every time you finish a client project to incorporate lessons learned.
content.next_steps: Once you have your starter kit running, deploy a 'Hello World' SaaS app to Vercel or Railway to verify your pipeline. Then, begin customizing the theme to match your freelance brand identity.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the same bugs over and over. Build once, deploy many, and charge for the value, not the hours spent debugging. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
